title: BE 5 `as unknown as` cleanup → helpers typés

correctness.notes: All 5 sites cleanly replace the type-loss casts with semantically equivalent constructs. Site 1: `as never` is sound — TS-narrowing-correct, no runtime change, branch unreachable by Promise.race ordering. Site 2: shallow spread `{ ...metadata }` lifts ChatAssistantMetadata to Record<string, unknown> AND adds defensive-copy semantics (provider mutation cannot leak back). Site 3: `parsedToJsonb` rewrite handles null + non-null + ref-inequality contract with explicit doc-string update. Site 4: `hasPgPool` predicate validates every numeric counter (totalCount, idleCount, waitingCount) — strictly stronger than the prior implicit cast that assumed presence. Site 5: Zod schema injection on RedisSocialOtcStore. Poisoned-cache blob now triggers safeParse() failure → fallback to in-memory consume() (not a leaked unknown payload). InMemorySocialOtcStore unchanged (no Redis-side parse), so backward compatible with the auth composition root that uses InMemory directly. Outer try/catch unchanged (Redis I/O errors still degrade to fallback).

tests.notes: 5 new test cases + 1 enriched suite; all 6 suites pass (88 tests). (1) `semaphore.test.ts` +1: 'short-circuits the inner runner when slot frees AFTER timeout' — uses real timers + blocker pattern + setImmediate flush to deterministically prove the deferred runner never invokes task() (asserts p2TaskRan === false). Excellent branch coverage. (2) `guardrail-evaluation-service.test.ts` +1: validates (a) Record-indexability of forwarded keys, (b) ref-inequality (spread semantics), (c) immutability of caller metadata via JSON.stringify snapshot — covers all 3 contracts of the inline spread. (3) `typeorm-museum-enrichment-cache.adapter.test.ts` NEW (3 cases): INSERT non-null/null + UPDATE path — fake repo captures saved rows, asserts ref-inequality + key parity + null pass-through. Indirect coverage (helper is module-internal, no export) is appropriate. (4) `start-pool-monitor.test.ts` NEW (10 cases): exhaustive predicate matrix (null, primitives, missing master, wrong-typed master, missing/non-numeric counters, narrowing compile-test) + 1 smoke test on interval handle shape. Cleanup via clearInterval prevents leak. (5) `social-otc-store.test.ts` +3 (RedisSocialOtcStore suite NEW): happy-path schema accept, poisoned-blob schema reject + atomic getdel removal, no-entry-no-fallback. Fake Redis stub minimal but sufficient. Minor nit: start-pool-monitor.test.ts:79 uses `as unknown as { ref?: unknown }` in a test (acceptable in test scope, not src — no policy violation).

kiss_dry.notes: Helpers stay co-localized per spec recommendation (no premature shared/utils/type-helpers.ts extraction). Site 1: 1-line edit, no helper. Site 2: inline spread instead of extracted `metadataToRecord` helper — justified because (a) single call-site, (b) helper would add indirection without reuse benefit, (c) the spec explicitly allowed `inline spread` as the green-agent variant. The 4-line comment above the spread documents the semantic upgrade (variance + defensive copy). guardrail-evaluation.service.ts is 611 lines (not the 400 mentioned in green-agent notes), but max-lines isn't enforced at 400 here — lint passes anyway, so inline-vs-extract is purely a KISS judgment, and the green-agent picked the simpler path. Site 3: parsedToJsonb rewrite removes the cast AND upgrades doc-string. Site 4: PgPoolStats interface + hasPgPool predicate are co-localized in data-source.ts — predicate exported for testability (only caller outside the module is the test file, confirmed via grep). Site 5: schema field added to existing ctor options bag (no new class hierarchy), createSocialOtcStore signature now requires `schema` only when Redis path is taken — InMemory fallback skips it cleanly. No DRY violations introduced.

security_honesty.notes: Site 5 (social-otc-store Zod) is a defense-in-depth UPGRADE: a poisoned cache entry (post-deploy shape drift, Redis MITM, attacker write) used to silently round-trip as `TPayload` and leak into RedeemSocialOtcUseCase. Now `safeParse` fails, the entry is dropped via getdel, the fallback in-memory store is tried, and a null return signals 'unredeemable code' to the caller. Hard security improvement. Site 4 (hasPgPool predicate) similarly upgrades runtime safety: the prior cast assumed `master.totalCount` was numeric; the predicate now validates all 3 counters are numbers before reading them, eliminating a NaN risk in the warn log payload. Honesty (UFR-013): all 5 fixes verified by reading the modified line in context + grep confirming `as unknown as` and `as TPayload` are GONE from src (only present in comments documenting the historical pattern). Tests run + pass verbatim — output below: `Test Suites: 6 passed, 6 total | Tests: 88 passed, 88 total`. No fabrication. No new `eslint-disable` introduced (grep `^\+.*eslint-disable` on diff returns 0).

a11y_design.notes: N/A — backend-only refactor (no UI surface). Default 95 per review guidelines.

findings.claim: `return undefined as never;` replaces `return undefined as unknown as T;`. Comment block (lines 87-90) explains the unreachable-path rationale (Promise.race already settled with rejected timeoutPromise).
findings.verified_via: Read file_path:semaphore.ts offset:80 limit:20 — confirmed verbatim.

findings.claim: `metadata: { ...metadata }` shallow spread replaces `metadata as unknown as Record<string, unknown>`. Inline (no helper extracted) — justified by single-call-site + KISS; semantic upgrade (defensive copy) documented in inline comment.
`parsedToJsonb` rewritten as `value === null ? null : { ...value }`. Doc-string upgraded (lines 148-156) to describe defensive-copy semantics + explicitly note the cast is gone.

Exported `hasPgPool(driver: unknown): driver is { master: PgPoolStats }` predicate added with strict runtime validation of all 3 counters. `startPoolMonitor` refactored to use predicate + early-return pattern (try/catch removed since predicate now validates the shape before reading).
findings.verified_via: git diff + grep confirming `hasPgPool` only consumed by data-source.ts:121 + new test file (no external src callers).

findings.claim: RedisSocialOtcStore ctor now requires `{ schema: ZodType<TPayload> }`. consume() runs `schema.safeParse(JSON.parse(value))` and falls through to fallback on parse failure. createSocialOtcStore signature updated; only call-site outside module is the test file (composition root uses InMemorySocialOtcStore directly — backward compatible since InMemory unchanged).

New test 'short-circuits the inner runner when slot frees AFTER timeout already fired' deterministically proves `task()` is never invoked when `timedOut === true` via blocker promise + setImmediate flush. Covers the `as never` branch verbatim.
New test validates 3 contracts of the inline spread: (1) Record-indexability, (2) ref-inequality, (3) caller metadata immutability via JSON.stringify snapshot diff.

Indirect coverage of internal `parsedToJsonb` via upsert INSERT (non-null + null) + UPDATE paths. Fake repo + DataSource pattern, captures `save` calls to assert ref-inequality + key parity. parsedToJsonb is module-internal — indirect coverage is the only viable approach.

Exhaustive predicate matrix: null/undefined/primitives/object-without-master/master-null/master-non-object/missing-counters/wrong-typed-counter/fully-shaped/narrowing-compile-test + 1 lifecycle smoke. clearInterval cleanup prevents handle leak.

New RedisSocialOtcStore suite (3 cases): schema-accept happy path, schema-reject poisoned blob + atomic getdel removal verification, no-entry null return. Fake Redis stub (Map-backed) sufficient for branch coverage.

verifications.outcome: 10 files changed, 238 insertions(+), 33 deletions(-) — matches expected scope (5 src + 3 modified test + 2 new test)

verifications.check: grep -rn 'as unknown as' museum-backend/src --include='*.ts' | grep -v migrations
verifications.outcome: 4 hits, all in doc-comments (semaphore.ts:90, guardrail.service.ts:498, typeorm-museum-enrichment-cache.adapter.ts:155, data-source.ts:99) documenting the PRIOR cast — no executable code remains. Zero src code casts.
2 hits, both in doc-comments (social-otc-store.ts:104, social-otc-store.ts:151) documenting the prior cast — no executable code remains.

verifications.check: cd museum-backend && pnpm lint (eslint + lint:test-discipline + tsc --noEmit)
verifications.outcome: PASS — no output errors, exit 0. ESLint --max-warnings=0 enforced on both src/ and tests/.
Test Suites: 6 passed, 6 total | Tests: 88 passed, 88 total — all T1.7 suites green. Per-test-run coverage threshold failure is expected (subset scope) and orthogonal to test pass/fail.
verifications.exit_code: 0 for tests (1 for jest global coverage gate which is OOS for this scope)

verifications.outcome: 0 new eslint-disable introduced — T1.7 contract respected.
Only 2 callers: data-source.ts:121 (intra-module) + tests/unit/data/db/start-pool-monitor.test.ts (test). No undesired external src caller. Export discipline respected.

verifications.check: grep callers of createSocialOtcStore / new RedisSocialOtcStore
verifications.outcome: Composition root (src/modules/auth/useCase/index.ts:117) uses `new InMemorySocialOtcStore` directly — no schema arg needed, unchanged signature. createSocialOtcStore only called from test. No prod call-site breakage.

summary: T1.7 ships cleanly. All 5 sites eliminated (4 `as unknown as` + 1 `as TPayload`) with semantically equivalent or stronger constructs (Site 4 hasPgPool predicate strictly tightens runtime validation; Site 5 Zod schema adds defense-in-depth against poisoned-cache leaks). Helpers stay co-localized per KISS doctrine; the green-agent's choice of inline spread at Site 2 (instead of extracted helper) is justified by single-call-site + clear inline doc. Tests are deterministic, comprehensive (88 pass), and cover both happy + fallback paths for every site. Lint + sentinel + tests all green. No eslint-disable. No external src caller breakage. weightedMean=95.05 → APPROVED.
